evals.name: scripts-clean-baseline
evals.description: Five evidence-locked rows that exercise edge cases (range conversion, °F conversion, calculated value, very high mp, very low bp). All should pass every sanity check.

evals.name: scripts-detect-seeded-errors
evals.description: Eleven rows with deliberately seeded errors of distinct types — bare code, truncated name, value out of range, wrong arithmetic, placeholder citation, empty name, implausible SMILES, intra-paper duplicate, plus two truncated-indicated-hydrogen-locant cases ('H-Indeno...' and 'H-Pyrrolo...' that should be '7H-Indeno...' / '1H-Pyrrolo...'). Run_all_checks should flag every error.

evals.name: csv-quote-lint-detects-unquoted-commas
evals.description: A 3-row fixture where row 2 has an unquoted comma in the compound_name field (N-(2,5-Dimethyl...). csv_quote_lint.py should detect this by column count mismatch OR by data_type carrying an unexpected value. Row 1 (no commas) and row 3 (commas properly quoted) should pass.

evals.name: validate-compound-name-detects-truncated-locant
evals.description: Specific check: 'H-Indeno[2,1-c]Quinolin-7-One' and 'H-Pyrrolo[3,2-d]pyrimidine' should be flagged as truncated-indicated-hydrogen-locant — real names are '7H-Indeno...' and '1H-Pyrrolo...' with a leading digit before the H. The fixture lives in datatypes/mp_bp/evals/files/ because the rest of seeded_errors.csv is mp/bp-shaped, but the lint itself is generic chemistry-name shape.

evals.name: value-range-helium-tungsten-edge
evals.description: Values at the extremes of the plausible range — helium bp ~ -269 °C, tungsten mp ~ 3422 °C — should NOT be flagged.

evals.name: unit-conversion-arithmetic-K
evals.description: Verify K → °C arithmetic on a clean baseline (Camostat 492.478 K → 219.328 °C). Should pass.

evals.name: unit-conversion-arithmetic-F
evals.description: Verify °F → °C arithmetic on a clean baseline (tungsten 6191.6 °F → 3422 °C). Should pass.

evals.name: validate-compound-name-flags-bare-code
evals.description: validate_compound_name.py flags 'compound 3' and '5-acetyl-' as invalid names. Generic chemistry-name shape rule.

evals.name: dedup-within-paper-flags-duplicate
evals.description: dedup_within_paper.py flags two rows in seeded_errors.csv (rows 8 and 9) as duplicates within the same paper.

evals.name: INACCESSIBLE-handling
evals.description: Agent given a paper directory with no readable files must flag with `flagged_paper_unreadable` rather than emit fabricated rows. (Agent-based eval — needs the skill-creator harness.)
evals.instructions: Apply the EXTRACTION_PROMPT_TEMPLATES.md Template 1 to a paper subdirectory containing only an empty article.pdf. The agent must NOT emit any data rows. It should emit either zero rows or one row with verification_status=flagged_review and notes containing flagged_paper_unreadable.

evals.name: extract-from-known-paper-with-provenance
evals.description: Apply the EXTRACTION_PROMPT_TEMPLATES.md Template 1 to a known paper subdirectory. Confirm: every emitted row has a non-empty evidence_quote; the quote is verbatim present in the paper file; meas_calc is correctly measured vs calculated. Use a known paper from the supported data type; mp/bp known papers are in trials/trial5/opus47/ (Trial-5 corpus).
evals.instructions: Apply Template 1 to {known_paper_subdir} with the appropriate datatype overlay. After extraction, run scripts/run_all_checks.py --datatype <X> with --paper-root. Expected: zero flags.

evals.name: verifier-prompt-detects-fabrication
evals.description: Independent verifier correctly flags a row whose evidence_quote is not in the paper file.
evals.instructions: Build a CSV with one correct row plus one row whose evidence_quote is a plausible-sounding string that doesn't actually appear in the paper. Apply VERIFICATION_PROMPT_TEMPLATES.md to both rows. The verifier must verify the first and flag the second with flagged_evidence_quote_not_found.

evals.name: v15-quote-support-lint-detects-missing-value-in-quote
evals.description: v1.5: quote_support_lint should hard-fail on rows where the quote is verbatim present in the paper but does not contain the numeric value (the 'Dark red solid;' Opus row 80 failure mode, and the GPT-5.5 masthead failure mode).
expected_stdout_contains: row 4: evidence_quote does not contain any numeric token
row 5: evidence_quote does not contain any numeric token

evals.name: v16-quote-template-lint-deprecated
evals.description: v1.6: quote_template_lint.py is a deprecated no-op. Confirm it exits 0 with a deprecation notice. Ellipsis and template-quote checks were removed because they affected verifiability (Tier 2), not correctness (Tier 1), and forced agents to drop correct rows.

evals.name: v16-compound-name-EA-prefix-detection
evals.description: v1.6: validate_compound_name flags rows with elemental-analysis line prepended to the compound name (e.g., 'C, 50.73; H, 5.72; N, 8.42. (IUPAC name)'). Caught 3 of T3 Opus's original failures and ~140 contaminated rows across the full corpus. (Reclassified as generic in v2.0: EA-prefix contamination can leak from any characterization table, not only mp/bp ones.)
expected_stdout_contains: row 16: compound_name has elemental-analysis prefix contamination

evals.name: v16-compound-name-leading-code-prefix-detection
evals.description: v1.6: validate_compound_name flags rows where the compound_name field starts with a paper-local code prefix like '6 (IUPAC name)'. This is the Phase 6e leading-context-prefix pattern; restored as an active lint in v1.6.
expected_stdout_contains: row 17: compound_name starts with a paper-local code prefix

evals.name: v16-compound-name-procedure-at-start-detection
evals.description: v1.6: validate_compound_name flags rows where compound_name begins with procedure-text or appearance description (Yield, White, Yellow, solid, powder, etc.) rather than chemistry.

evals.name: v15-compound-name-shape-expanded
evals.description: v1.5: validate_compound_name extends to terminal unfinished-suffix tokens (Carboxamid), dangling hyphen/prime tails, unbalanced parens, narrow procedure-text contamination. Crucially: must NOT false-positive on legitimate parenthesized stereo descriptors (E)-, (S,S)-, (-)-.
expected_stdout_contains: row 9: compound_name ends in unfinished suffix token
row 10: compound_name ends with a dangling hyphen/prime

evals.name: v20-conversion-arithmetic-lint-shapes
evals.description: v2.0: conversion_arithmetic_lint.py accepts the three SKILL.md example shapes (K→°C, °F→°C, redox SCE→SHE) plus their ASCII-operator equivalents, and rejects four malformed shapes (missing =, missing operator, missing units, missing input number).

evals.name: v15-stereo-descriptors-not-flagged
evals.description: v1.5: legitimate IUPAC stereo / optical descriptors that start with '(' must NOT be flagged by validate_compound_name. Rows 13-15 of v15_seeded_errors are (E)-, (S,S)-, and (-)- patterns and should pass through cleanly. This is a guard against the false-positive pattern observed in the initial v1.5 draft (which had a too-broad leading-paren rule).
